response: The three main psychedelic compounds found in Psilocybin genus mushrooms that are converted to psilocin by the body are psilocybin, psilocin, and baeocystin. Psilocybin is converted to psilocin through metabolic processes in the body, while psilocin remains unchanged. Baeocystin is an "N"-demethylated derivative of psilocybin and is a phosphorylated derivative of 4-HO-NMT, but it is not converted to psilocin. Therefore, only psilocybin is converted to psilocin by the body.
